message:
🌱 Add cilium: LB-IPAM: Unable to set single addresses as a pool mission (#1383)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cilium-24351-lb-ipam-unable-to-set-single-addresses-as-a-pool",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cilium: LB-IPAM: Unable to set single addresses as a pool",
+    "description": "LB-IPAM: Unable to set single addresses as a pool. This issue affects 18+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify cilium troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `"provided IP is not in the valid range. The range of valid IPs is 185.218.193.58/32" subsys=lbipam`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review cilium configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### What happened?

I tried to create a pool with only single IPv4 (/32) and IPv6 (/128) addresses inside it."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for LB-IPAM: Unable to set single addresses as a pool",
+        "description": "This PR introduces a new IP allocator, replaces the existing IP allocator used by LB-IPAM with this new allocator. This removes the limitation on IPv6 ranges, the full range can now be used (only limited by the resources required to book keep). It also removes the reservation of the base and
```yaml

    "com
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm LB-IPAM: Unable to set single addresses as a pool is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `"provided IP is not in the valid range. The range of valid IPs is 185.218.193.58/32" subsys=lbipam` no longer appears in logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This PR introduces a new IP allocator, replaces the existing IP allocator used by LB-IPAM with this new allocator. This removes the limitation on IPv6 ranges, the full range can now be used (only limited by the resources required to book keep). It also removes the reservation of the base and broadcast IP address making it possible to allocate single IPs.",

+        "{
  "clientVersion": {
